LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CORY OBERDORFER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO MAINTAIN HIS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BURDEN BY FAILING TO DEMONSTRATE THE AMOUNT OF HIS DAMAGES. SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING DOES NOT ALTER THE COURT'S DECISION. TO GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, PLAINTIFF MUST PRESENT EVIDENCE REGARDING EACH ELEMENT OF HIS CAUSE OF ACTION AND COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. (CCP SEC. 437C(F)(1) AND (P)1. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MAY BE GRANTED FOR A PLAINTIFF WHERE THE DAMAGES ARE ASCERTAINABLE AND CERTAIN, THUS AVOIDING A TRIAL. HERE, TRIAL WOULD NOT BE AVOIDED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN ON ISSUES 10 AND 11 BECAUSE HE DOES NOT SHOW HE COMPLIED WITH DEFENDANT'S GRIEVANCE PROCESS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Sets Three And Four; And Rquests To Supplement Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Pltfs Councel (Per Order)
Off Calendar Oct-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Both parties present at hearing, did not sign in or leave card.
After hearing, Defendant Robert Finkelstein, Rchelle Becker and The Utility Reform Networ's Motion to compel production of documents, sets three and four; and requests to supplement interrogatories and production of documents; and for imposition of monetary sanctions against plaintiff's counsel:
1. As to request for production of documents, set three: hearing required. The parties should be prepared to argue whether attorney's fees are an element of the damages sought by plaintiff- Granted on terms and conditions agreed by the parties.
2. As to request for production of documents, set four: within five days of notice of entry of this order, plaintiff shall produce any responsive documents identified by plaintiff - Granted on terms and conditions agreed by the parties.
3. As to defendants' supplemental interrogatory: plaintiff shall serve a verified supplemental response that sets forth any information, responsive to defendants' previous interrogatories, which has been acquired by plaintiff since plaintiff's initial responses; Granted, on terms and conditions agreed by the parties.
4. As to defendants' supplemental request for production of documents, denied;
5. Request for monetary sanctions: Granted in the amount of $390.00 payable within 30 days. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is Denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CORY OBERDORFER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Sangeeta Singal, Esq. of The Law Offices of Sangeeta Singal 515-3452 for pltf & moving party; Ronald S. Bushner, Esq. of Wilson Elser Moskowitz Edelman & Dicker LLP 433-0990 for defts & opposing parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion denied. Plaintiff fails to maintain his summary adjudication burden by failing to demonstrate the amount of his damages. Supplemental briefing does not alter the Court's decision. To grant summary adjudication, plaintiff must present evidence regarding each element of his cause of action and completely dispose of the cause of action. (CCP sec. 437c(f)(1) and (p)1. Summary adjudication may be granted for a plaintiff where the damages are ascertainable and certain, thus avoiding a trial. Here, trial would not be avoided. Plaintiff fails to shift the burden on issues 10 and 11 because he does not show he complied with defendant's grievance process. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353
